name: faster-whisper
description: Optimized Whisper inference (CTranslate2) for fast local transcription

status.comment: Actively maintained; latest release v1.2.1 on 2025-10-31.

primary-type.comment: A Python library/runtime a user runs themselves (CTranslate2-based Whisper inference engine); core job is file transcription. Not a packaged dictation or meeting product.

real-time-dictation.comment: Batch/streaming transcription library via Python API; no built-in speak-to-type dictation. Third-party apps add this on top.

file-transcription.comment: Core function: transcribe local audio/video files via the Python API (model.transcribe()). No FFmpeg system dependency required.

live-meeting-capture.comment: No meeting-bot or system-audio capture in the library itself.

streaming-low-latency.comment: Reference library processes audio in batch (with VAD filtering and batched inference for speed); low-latency live streaming is only available through community integrations, not the core library.

audio-sources.comment: Primary input is local audio files; microphone capture is achievable by scripting against the Python API.

underlying-engine.comment: A reimplementation of OpenAI's Whisper model running on CTranslate2, a fast inference engine for Transformer models. Up to ~4x faster than the reference implementation with lower memory use and optional 8-bit quantization.

language-count.comment: Approx 99 languages — inherits OpenAI Whisper's multilingual coverage since it runs the same model weights.

english-accuracy.comment: Runs the same Whisper model weights, so accuracy matches the reference Whisper (near state-of-the-art on clean English, single-digit WER for large models). Rated 4 like OpenAI Whisper; smaller/quantized models and noisy/overlapping speech degrade, and there is no built-in diarization.

multilingual-auto-detect.comment: Performs automatic spoken-language detection (returns detected language and probability); transcribes non-English audio without manual language selection.

custom-vocabulary.comment: Supports an initial_prompt parameter to bias recognition toward specific names/terms; not a managed dictionary but a real user-facing mechanism to add context terms.

speaker-diarization.comment: No built-in speaker diarization; requires external tools (e.g. pyannote, as in WhisperX) to label speakers.

timestamps.comment: Provides segment-level timestamps and optional word-level timestamps (word_timestamps=True).

ai-summaries-notes.comment: Pure transcription engine; produces no summaries or action items.

translation.comment: Supports Whisper's speech-translation task (task="translate"), which translates non-English audio into English text. Translation target is English only.

export-formats.comment: The library returns Python segment objects (text + timestamps) rather than writing files; programmatic output is naturally serialized to JSON. CLI subtitle/text exports (srt/vtt/txt) come from wrapper tools like whisper-ctranslate2, not the core library.

processing-location.comment: Runs entirely locally on CPU or NVIDIA GPU; model weights are downloaded and inference happens on the user's machine.

offline-capable.comment: Fully offline once model weights are downloaded; no network connection required for transcription.

data-used-for-training.comment: Self-hosted local inference; no vendor receives audio or transcripts, so user data is not used to train models.

data-deletion-control.comment: All inputs and outputs are local files fully under the user's control.

platforms.comment: Python package (3.9+) installable via pip; runs on macOS, Windows and Linux (CPU; NVIDIA GPU via CUDA). No mobile or web build from the reference repo.

system-wide-dictation.comment: Not a dictation tool; no OS-wide text injection.

integrations.comment: No meeting/app integrations in the library.

api-automation.comment: Provides a Python API (WhisperModel.transcribe) a power user can script against; CLI front-ends exist via community wrappers.

pricing-model.comment: Free and open-source (MIT); no subscription. Practical cost is local compute and setup effort.

free-allowance.comment: No usage cap; limited only by local hardware.

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Free/OSS with no subscription; only cost is local compute.
